Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Department Of Public Works' Motion For Summary Judgment - (Recusal Of Judge Peter J. Busch.)
Off Calendar Aug-23-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendant's Motion For Summary Judgment Re Derek Madaris' Claims.
Appearances: Craig K. Martin, Esquire (415.640.4803) Appearing For Plaintiff. Ruth M. Bond, Deputy City Attorney And Sallie P. Gibson, Deputy City Attorney (Office Of The City Attorney, 415.554.3876) Appearing For Defendant.
Ruling: Argued And The Court Adopts Its Tentative Ruling: granted. Plaintiff is unable to establish a prima facie case of race or national origin or gender discrimination or retaliation. Both white male and female employees were laid off as a part of the City's budget problems. Plaintiff was placed in an other job with the City as were the two female employees about whom plaintiff complains. Plaintiff contends that the females received a better job, but it was one without benefits and it was one that the females had prior experience doing. The decision to terminate plaintiffs was admittedly made by the head of the department, Ed Lee, and there is no evidence that his decision was based on race or sex or to retaliate; rather, it was based on the need to cut the budget. As a consequence, the derogatory comments of Montgomery and Monahan do not help Plaintiff establish a prima facie case. Plaintiff's loss of the library position admittedly was due to the end of a job he knew to be temporary.
Assuming Plaintiff establishes a prima facie case, the City has presented a legitimate, non-discriminatory reason for its actions, a budget crises, and legitimate reasons for its decision to appoint Monahan and Anderson to the street inspector position and legitimate reasons for the termination of the library position. Plaintiff cannot demonstrate that the reasons presented were a pretext to remove Plaintiff from his job. As to Plaintiff's claim of retaliation, it is based on speculation. The Court has ruled on defendant's evidentiary objections (copy of order given to respective counsel). Ms. Bond to submit a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge Mahoney, Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
